response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend web engineer with a broad multi-language background, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU and AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD microarchitecture, and DNN workload performance analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is writing low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby and some embedded/hardware-adjacent work with robotic arms at Altom, but these fall far short of the hardware-software co-design and silicon performance modeling skills the role requires.
strengths: Genuine low-latency and performance optimization experience in high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Broad systems-level exposure across embedded, native, and infrastructure layers over 15+ years
Recent AI-agent and developer-tooling work at re:cinq shows awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling or chip-level performance analysis experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization background whatsoever
Seniority and trajectory are firmly in web/full-stack engineering, not hardware or silicon performance engineering
